column1: Figure 1: Total Fertility Rate decreased in 2024 despite an increase in live births.
Total Fertility Rate and live birth counts, England and Wales, 1938 to 2024
Source: Births in England and Wales from the Office for National Statistics

column2: 1. The total fertility rate is the average number of live children that a group of women would bear if they experienced the age-specific fertility rates of the calendar year throughout their childbearing lifespan.

2. This graph contains revised TFRs for 2022 and 2023 following the revision of the mid-year population estimates for 2022 and 2023, therefore these rates may not align to the rates published in previous years.
Number of live births/ Total fertility rate
